title: Cutting blade and method for producing such a blade
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a cutting blade (3) for a clearing saw (1), the cutting blade (3) having a plurality of teeth (11), at least some of which comprise a leading edge (19), a trailing edge (21), and a point (17) in between the leading and trailing edges. The trailing edge (21) is hardened to a depth in the range 0.02-1.5 mm, and the leading edge (19) is left unhardened. This provides a cutting blade with increased durability and efficiency, that can still be filed manually to remain sharp.
claims:
Claims 1 . Cutting blade (3) for a clearing saw (1 ), the cutting blade (3) comprising a plurality of teeth (1 1 ), and characterized by at least some of said teeth (1 1 ) comprising a leading edge (19), a trailing edge (21 ), and a point (17) in between the leading edge (19) and the trailing edge (21 ), wherein the trailing edge (21 ) is hardened to a depth in the range 0.02-1 .5 mm, wherein the hardening depth is defined as the depth where the hardness has decreased to 590 HV, and the leading edge (19) is left unhardened except at said point. 2. Cutting blade according to claim 1 , wherein the trailing edge (21 ) is hardened to a depth in the range 0.02-0.5 mm. 3. Cutting blade according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the leading edge (19) is concave and faces the blade's (3) dedicated circumferential direction of rotation 4. Cutting blade according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the cutting blade teeth (1 1 ) are separated by intervening cut-outs (13). 5. Cutting blade according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the tooth comprises a bend (1 5) and is directed out of the plane of interior parts of the blade. 6. A method for producing a cutting blade (3) for a clearing saw (1 ), the cutting blade (3) comprising a plurality of teeth (1 1 ), and the method being characterized by providing at least some of said teeth (1 1 ) with a leading edge (19), a trailing edge (21 ), and a point (17) in between the leading edge (19) and the trailing edge (21 ), 

 hardening the trailing edge (21 ) to a depth in the range 0.02-1 .5 mm, wherein the hardening depth is defined as the depth where the hardness has decreased to 590 HV, and leaving the leading edge (19) unhardened. 7. Method according to claim 6, wherein the trailing edge (21 ) is hardened inductively. 8. Method according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the trailing edge (21 ) is quenched after hardening. 9. Method according to claim 8, wherein the teeth are subsequently tempered. 10. Method according to any of claims 6-9, wherein the trailing edge (21 ) is hardened to a depth in the range 0.02-0.5 mm. 1 1 . Method according to any of claims 6-10, wherein during the hardening the trailing edge (21 ) is initially hardened to a deeper depth, and is subsequently ground down until the hardening depth is in the range 0.02-1 .5 mm.
